expectations.text: AGENTS.md is created at the project root
expectations.evidence: outputs/AGENTS.md exists (1509 bytes). Also confirmed at /tmp/eval-legacy-node/AGENTS.md.

expectations.text: CLAUDE.md exists and is a symlink pointing to AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: /tmp/eval-legacy-node/CLAUDE.md is a symlink: 'lrwxr-xr-x CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md'

expectations.text: knowledge/ directory is created with at least one .md file
expectations.evidence: outputs/knowledge/ contains three .md files: order-lifecycle.md, payment-stripe-integration.md, module-boundaries.md

expectations.text: Knowledge doc filenames describe problem domains (e.g. order-lifecycle.md), not code modules (e.g. service-a.md)
expectations.evidence: Filenames are order-lifecycle.md, payment-stripe-integration.md, module-boundaries.md — all describe business/architectural domains, not code files or modules by file name.

expectations.text: Knowledge docs do not copy implementation details (no function bodies, SQL, or call sequences)
expectations.evidence: All three docs describe rules, constraints, and state transitions in prose/tables. No function bodies or SQL found. payment-stripe-integration.md mentions a call sequence (4-step flow) but at a conceptual level describing the integration pattern, not a code-level call sequence. No raw code or SQL is present.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md contains a table or links pointing to knowledge docs
expectations.evidence: AGENTS.md has a markdown table under '## Knowledge Base' with links to all three knowledge docs: knowledge/order-lifecycle.md, knowledge/payment-stripe-integration.md, knowledge/module-boundaries.md.

expectations.text: The 'only webhook sets PAID status' constraint is captured somewhere in the knowledge base
expectations.evidence: Captured in multiple places: (1) AGENTS.md Hard Constraints: '订单状态 PAID 只能由 Stripe Webhook 处理器写入，任何其他路径都是错误的'; (2) order-lifecycle.md state table: 'PAID 支付已确认（仅由 Stripe webhook 设置）'; (3) payment-stripe-integration.md: '订单状态 PAID 只能由 webhook 处理器写入，任何其他路径都是错误的' and Hard Constraints: '不得在 webhook 处理之外手动将订单状态设为 PAID'.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md is created at the monorepo root
expectations.evidence: outputs/AGENTS.md exists and contains a well-structured root-level AGENTS.md with a Packages table, Cross-Package Knowledge section, Hard Constraints, and Task Routing.

expectations.text: Root CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: ls -la /tmp/eval-monorepo/CLAUDE.md shows: lrwxr-xr-x CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md

expectations.text: packages/api/AGENTS.md is created
expectations.evidence: outputs/packages/api/AGENTS.md exists and contains @platform/api documentation with Knowledge Base, Hard Constraints, and Task Routing sections.

expectations.text: packages/api/CLAUDE.md is a symlink to packages/api/AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: ls -la /tmp/eval-monorepo/packages/api/CLAUDE.md shows: lrwxr-xr-x CLAUDE.md -> AGENTS.md

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md references or links to per-package AGENTS.md files
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md Packages table has '[→](packages/api/AGENTS.md)' for packages/api, and Task Routing includes 'Working inside packages/api? Start at [packages/api/AGENTS.md](packages/api/AGENTS.md)'.

expectations.text: packages/api/knowledge/ is created with at least one doc
expectations.evidence: outputs/packages/api/knowledge/ contains 3 documents: auth-token-verification.md, graphql-schema-design.md, and rate-limiting.md.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md covers cross-package concerns; api AGENTS.md focuses on api-specific content
expectations.evidence: Root AGENTS.md covers shared type contracts, auth boundaries, coordinated change protocol, and cross-package routing. packages/api/AGENTS.md focuses exclusively on JWT verification, GraphQL schema design, rate limiting, and resolver constraints, while correctly linking back to root for cross-package concerns (e.g., '../../knowledge/shared-contracts.md').

expectations.text: knowledge/order-lifecycle.md was modified (not left unchanged)
expectations.evidence: File was clearly modified: new REFUND_PENDING state, updated transitions, new business rules, and a footer timestamp noting the change.

expectations.text: REFUND_PENDING state appears in the updated doc
expectations.evidence: REFUND_PENDING appears in the States table, transition rules, business rules, and a dedicated 'Open Questions' section.

expectations.text: Both CANCELLED→REFUND_PENDING and REFUND_PENDING→CANCELLED transitions are documented
expectations.evidence: Both CANCELLED → REFUND_PENDING and REFUND_PENDING → CANCELLED appear explicitly in the Allowed Transitions block.

expectations.text: Doc notes that CANCELLED is no longer a terminal state (this is a breaking change in the mental model)
expectations.evidence: Called out in 3 places: Key Business Rules ('CANCELLED is no longer a terminal state'), Common Mistakes ('Treating CANCELLED as a terminal state'), and the footer.

expectations.text: Doc does NOT invent specific business semantics for REFUND_PENDING (e.g. '30-day window', 'triggered by customer') without a TODO marker indicating it needs confirmation
expectations.evidence: No invented facts stated as truth. The REFUND_PENDING description is deliberately neutral. All uncertain semantics are in a 'TODO: requires clarification' section.

expectations.text: Unclear business semantics are marked as TODO or question, not silently assumed
expectations.evidence: Five explicit TODO items cover: what triggers the transition, what processing occurs, resolution conditions, timeout/SLA, and eligibility. Nothing is silently assumed.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md is created at the project root
expectations.evidence: AGENTS.md exists at outputs/AGENTS.md (1402 bytes). It contains a project overview, a knowledge index table, key rules, and known legacy issues.

expectations.text: CLAUDE.md exists and is a symlink pointing to AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: /tmp/eval-legacy-node-baseline/CLAUDE.md does not exist. `ls -la /tmp/eval-legacy-node-baseline/` shows only AGENTS.md, knowledge/, package.json, README.md, and src/ — no CLAUDE.md at all.

expectations.text: knowledge/ directory is created with at least one .md file
expectations.evidence: outputs/knowledge/ contains three .md files: architecture.md (1797 bytes), order-module.md (2560 bytes), payment-module.md (2034 bytes).

expectations.text: Knowledge doc filenames describe problem domains (e.g. order-lifecycle.md), not code modules (e.g. service-a.md)
expectations.evidence: The filenames are: architecture.md, order-module.md, payment-module.md. 'order-module' and 'payment-module' directly reference code modules (the src/order/ and src/payment/ directories). Better problem-domain names would be 'order-lifecycle.md' and 'payment-processing.md'. 'architecture.md' is acceptable as it describes a concern, not a file.

expectations.text: Knowledge docs do not copy implementation details (no function bodies, SQL, or call sequences)
expectations.evidence: None of the three docs contain function bodies or SQL. payment-module.md has a textual flow diagram (ASCII arrows) describing the PaymentIntent flow, but this describes the conceptual process rather than copying code. No raw function signatures, no SQL queries, and no verbatim call sequences are present. The state machine in order-module.md is represented as a table of transitions, not as code.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md contains a table or links pointing to knowledge docs
expectations.evidence: AGENTS.md lines 11-15 contain a markdown table with three rows, each linking to a knowledge doc: knowledge/architecture.md, knowledge/order-module.md, knowledge/payment-module.md, each with a description of the content.

expectations.text: The 'only webhook sets PAID status' constraint is captured somewhere in the knowledge base
expectations.evidence: Captured in three places: (1) AGENTS.md rule #2: 'only Stripe Webhook can set PAID status'; (2) order-module.md rule #2: 'PAID status can only be written by Stripe Webhook'; (3) payment-module.md: 'only this Webhook handler can update order status to PAID, no other code path is allowed to manually write PAID'.

expectations.text: AGENTS.md is created at the monorepo root
expectations.evidence: No AGENTS.md file exists in outputs/. The outputs directory contains only knowledge/ and packages/ subdirectories. No AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md at any level.

expectations.text: Root CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline/CLAUDE.md does not exist. `ls -la /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline/` shows only: knowledge/, package.json, packages/, README.md — no CLAUDE.md symlink.

expectations.text: packages/api/AGENTS.md is created
expectations.evidence: outputs/packages/api/ contains only a knowledge/ subdirectory. No AGENTS.md present.

expectations.text: packages/api/CLAUDE.md is a symlink to packages/api/AGENTS.md
expectations.evidence: /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline/packages/api/CLAUDE.md does not exist. The api directory contains only: knowledge/, package.json, src/.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md references or links to per-package AGENTS.md files
expectations.evidence: No AGENTS.md exists at any location in outputs/ or /tmp/eval-monorepo-baseline/. Cannot satisfy this expectation.

expectations.text: packages/api/knowledge/ is created with at least one doc
expectations.evidence: outputs/packages/api/knowledge/overview.md exists (827 chars) with genuine content: GraphQL API overview, auth (JWT), rate limiting, and dependency notes on @platform/shared.

expectations.text: Root AGENTS.md covers cross-package concerns; api AGENTS.md focuses on api-specific content
expectations.evidence: No AGENTS.md files were created anywhere. The executor created knowledge/ docs but none of these are AGENTS.md files.

expectations.text: knowledge/order-lifecycle.md was modified (not left unchanged)
expectations.evidence: The output file contains REFUND_PENDING which was not in the original (original had only PENDING, PAID, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED). The file also includes a footer: 'Last updated: 2026-04-20 | Reason: Added REFUND_PENDING state; updated CANCELLED transition rules (no longer terminal)'

expectations.text: REFUND_PENDING state appears in the updated doc
expectations.evidence: REFUND_PENDING appears in the States table with description 'Refund request initiated, awaiting processing', in the Allowed Transitions block, and in the Key Business Rules section.

expectations.text: Both CANCELLED→REFUND_PENDING and REFUND_PENDING→CANCELLED transitions are documented
expectations.evidence: Allowed Transitions block contains: 'CANCELLED → REFUND_PENDING' and 'REFUND_PENDING → CANCELLED'. Both transitions are also explained in Key Business Rules.

expectations.text: Doc notes that CANCELLED is no longer a terminal state (this is a breaking change in the mental model)
expectations.evidence: Common Mistakes section explicitly states: 'Treating CANCELLED as terminal — it can now transition to REFUND_PENDING.' The footer also states 'updated CANCELLED transition rules (no longer terminal)'.

expectations.text: Doc does NOT invent specific business semantics for REFUND_PENDING (e.g. '30-day window', 'triggered by customer') without a TODO marker indicating it needs confirmation
expectations.evidence: The doc states concrete invented semantics without any TODO markers. Key Business Rules includes: 'CANCELLED → REFUND_PENDING: a cancelled order can enter refund processing if a refund is requested after cancellation' and 'REFUND_PENDING → CANCELLED: if the refund is rejected or withdrawn, the order returns to CANCELLED'. The specific triggers ('requested after cancellation', 'rejected or withdrawn') were not provided in the diff and are stated as facts rather than flagged with TODO.

expectations.text: Unclear business semantics are marked as TODO or question, not silently assumed
expectations.evidence: No TODO markers appear anywhere in the file. The doc invents specific business logic for REFUND_PENDING transitions and states them as established facts rather than flagging them as uncertain details needing confirmation.
